title: Test: Multiple and varied identifier formats
description:
Edge case for different identifier formats in MQTT discovery.

Test scenarios:
- Single identifier: {"identifiers": ["device_123"]}
- Multiple identifiers: {"identifiers": ["mac_addr", "serial_num"]}
- Tuple format: {"identifiers": [["mqtt", "device_123"]]}
- Mixed formats in same payload
- Identifier that doesn't match any device
- Identifier that matches multiple devices (should this be possible?)

Questions to resolve:
- Does HA's async_get_device() want (domain, id) tuples or just strings?
- Do we need to try multiple identifier formats?
- What domain do MQTT-discovered devices use? 'mqtt'?

Implementation notes:
- Check how HA MQTT integration formats identifiers in device registry
- Match that format exactly
- Log clearly when no match found

title: Test: Area does not exist yet
description:
Edge case where suggested_area references an area that hasn't been created in HA.

Test scenarios:
- Discovery with suggested_area='New Room' where 'New Room' doesn't exist
- Verify area is created via area_registry.async_create()
- Verify device is assigned to newly created area
- Verify case sensitivity handling (is 'kitchen' == 'Kitchen'?)
- Verify special characters in area names work
- Verify very long area names are handled (truncated? rejected?)

Implementation notes:
- Need to look up area by name first: area_registry.async_get_area_by_name()
- If None, create it
- HA might normalize area names - need to match that behavior
close_reason: Project complete - integration implemented, tested, published

title: Test: Discovery arrives before device registration
description:
Edge case where MQTT discovery message is processed before HA has finished registering the device from the same discovery.

Test scenarios:
- Send discovery, immediately check registry (should queue for retry)
- Verify retry succeeds after device appears
- Verify retry gives up after max attempts with appropriate logging
- Verify queue doesn't grow unbounded with repeated discoveries

Implementation notes:
- Need mock that delays device registration
- Check that we don't duplicate updates when retry succeeds

title: Implement device registry updates
description:
Look up devices and apply metadata updates:
- Use dr.async_get_device() with MQTT identifiers
- Call dr.async_update_device() for area_id and/or name_by_user
- Use ar.async_get_area_by_name() to resolve area names to IDs
- Create areas that don't exist (matches Z-Wave behavior)

Depends on MQTT subscription being complete.

title: Implement ha-mqtt-device-sync integration
description: Home Assistant custom integration that syncs device metadata (area, name) from MQTT discovery payloads to the device registry. Addresses HA's limitation where suggested_area only works on first discovery.
close_reason: Project complete - integration implemented, tested, published

title: Feature: Parse area from device name convention
description:
Fallback heuristic: if no suggested_area in discovery payload, try to parse it from device name.

Convention: 'Area / Device Name' or 'Area - Device Name'

Examples:
- 'Living Room / Plug' → area='Living Room', name='Plug'
- 'Kitchen / Ceiling Light' → area='Kitchen', name='Ceiling Light'
- 'Garage - Door Sensor' → area='Garage', name='Door Sensor'

Config options:
- name_delimiter: string, default ' / ' (space-slash-space)
- parse_area_from_name: bool, default false (opt-in, don't surprise users)
- update_device_name: bool - also set the parsed name? (probably yes if we're parsing)

Edge cases:
- No delimiter in name → no-op, leave as-is
- Multiple delimiters ('A / B / C') → first split only, area='A', name='B / C'
- Empty area after split → skip
- Empty name after split → skip
- Delimiter at start/end → skip

This enables Tasmota users (and anyone else) to get area assignment without upstream changes. Just rename devices to follow the convention.

Lower priority than explicit suggested_area - only use as fallback.

title: Test: HA restart and retained messages
description:
Edge case for Home Assistant restart behavior.

Test scenarios:
- HA restarts, retained discovery messages replayed by broker
- Verify we process retained messages on startup
- Verify we don't re-apply areas that are already correct (no-op)
- Verify startup doesn't block on slow MQTT broker
- Integration loaded before MQTT integration - handle gracefully
- Integration loaded after MQTT - should still work

Implementation notes:
- MQTT messages with retain=true are replayed on subscribe
- Our subscription happens at integration load
- Need to handle the 'burst' of retained messages on startup
- async_setup_entry should not block waiting for messages
close_reason: Project complete - integration implemented, tested, published

title: Test: Rapid-fire discovery updates
description:
Edge case for high-frequency discovery message bursts.

Test scenarios:
- Same device sends discovery 10x in 1 second (e.g., on reconnect)
- 100 different devices send discovery simultaneously (HA restart with Z2M)
- Same device, area changes rapidly (A -> B -> C in <1s)
- Verify we don't spam device_registry with redundant updates
- Verify we don't create race conditions

Implementation notes:
- Debounce per-device? Or just check if value already matches?
- async_update_device should be idempotent but verify
- Consider batching updates if volume is high
- Log at debug level for each message, info level for actual changes
close_reason: Project complete - integration implemented, tested, published

title: Test: Malformed and edge-case payloads
description:
Edge case for unexpected MQTT discovery payload formats.

Test scenarios:
- Empty payload (birth/will message clearing retained config)
- Invalid JSON
- Valid JSON but missing 'device' key
- 'device' present but no 'identifiers'
- 'device' present but no 'suggested_area' (should be no-op)
- suggested_area is empty string
- suggested_area is null
- suggested_area is not a string (number, object, array)
- Very large payload (1MB+)
- Unicode in suggested_area ('Küche', '客厅', emoji)

Implementation notes:
- Graceful handling for all cases - log and skip, never crash
- Use try/except around JSON parsing
- Validate types before using
close_reason: Project complete - integration implemented, tested, published

title: Test: Custom discovery prefix
description:
Edge case for non-standard MQTT discovery prefixes.

Test scenarios:
- Default prefix 'homeassistant' works
- Custom prefix 'my_ha' works (Z2M allows this)
- Prefix with trailing slash handled correctly
- Prefix change at runtime (restart required? hot reload?)
- Multiple prefixes simultaneously (is this a use case?)

Implementation notes:
- Config option: discovery_prefix (default 'homeassistant')
- Subscribe pattern: {prefix}/+/+/config
- Document that this must match HA's MQTT integration setting

title: Test: Overwrite behavior and user customizations
description:
Edge case for respecting vs overwriting user-set areas.

Test scenarios:
- Device has no area, discovery has suggested_area -> should apply
- Device has area set by user, discovery has different suggested_area, overwrite=false -> should NOT apply
- Device has area set by user, discovery has different suggested_area, overwrite=true -> should apply
- Device area was set by US previously, discovery has new area -> should apply (we're the source of truth)
- How do we know if area was set by user vs by us?

Implementation notes:
- May need to track 'last area we set' per device
- Or check if current area matches last suggested_area we saw
- Config option: overwrite_existing (default false)
- Consider adding 'managed_by' metadata if HA supports it
close_reason: Project complete - integration implemented, tested, published

title: Test: Device name sync behavior
description:
Edge case for optional device name synchronization.

Test scenarios:
- sync_name=false (default): name in discovery ignored
- sync_name=true: device.name applied to device registry
- Name contains special characters, unicode, emoji
- Name is very long (100+ chars)
- Name is empty string
- Name conflicts with existing device name
- Name changes frequently (should we sync every time?)

Implementation notes:
- Device registry field is 'name_by_user' for user-set names
- Need to verify this is the right field to update
- Default OFF because name sync is more invasive than area sync
- User might have deliberately renamed device in HA
